title: The Singleton design pattern should be used with care
description: The Singleton pattern restricts the instantiation of a class to one object. While it can be useful in some scenarios, overusing or misusing this pattern can lead to issues such as hidden dependencies, difficulties in testing, and problems with concurrency. Consider alternatives before applying the Singleton pattern.
recommendation: Avoid using the Singleton pattern unless it is absolutely necessary. Prefer dependency injection or other design patterns that promote better testability and maintainability.
impact: Overuse of the Singleton pattern can make code harder to test, maintain, and extend. It can also introduce hidden dependencies and concurrency issues.

checks.message: Avoid using the Singleton pattern unless it is absolutely necessary. Prefer dependency injection or other design patterns that promote better testability and maintainability.
